question: What is the minimum attendance required at GNITS?
answer: Students must maintain a minimum of 75% attendance in each subject to be eligible for semester end examinations.

question: What happens if my attendance falls below 75%?
answer: Students with less than 75% attendance will not be permitted to appear in semester end examinations for that subject.

question: Can attendance shortage be condoned at GNITS?
answer: Yes. Students with attendance between 65% and 75% can apply for condonation with valid medical or other genuine reasons. Maximum 10% condonation is granted.

question: How is attendance calculated at GNITS?
answer: Attendance is calculated separately for theory and laboratory subjects from the first day of class to the last working day of the semester.

question: Can parents view my attendance online?
answer: Yes. Parents and students can view attendance on the GNITS ERP portal at gnits.ac.in/erp. Automated SMS is sent when attendance falls below 80%.

question: How many internal exams are there per semester?
answer: There are two mid-term exams per semester. First mid is after the first half of the syllabus and second mid is after the complete syllabus.

question: What is the marks distribution for subjects at GNITS?
answer: Each subject has 30 marks for internal assessment and 70 marks for the JNTUH semester end examination making a total of 100 marks.

question: What is the minimum marks to pass in semester exams?
answer: Students must score a minimum of 35 out of 70 marks in the JNTUH semester end examination to pass a subject.

question: What grading system does GNITS follow?
answer: GNITS follows the JNTUH CBCS (Choice Based Credit System) grading system with grades ranging from O (Outstanding) to F (Fail).

question: What is the grade for scoring 91 to 100 marks?
answer: Students scoring 91 to 100 marks receive the O (Outstanding) grade which carries 10 grade points.

question: What grade do I get if I score between 81 and 90?
answer: Scoring 81 to 90 marks gives you an A+ (Excellent) grade which carries 9 grade points.

question: How is CGPA calculated at GNITS?
answer: CGPA is calculated as the sum of (Credit × Grade Points for all semesters) divided by the total number of credits earned.

question: What is SGPA?
answer: SGPA is Semester Grade Point Average. It is calculated as the sum of (Credit × Grade Points) for that semester divided by the total credits in that semester.

question: When are semester end exams conducted?
answer: Odd semester exams are in November/December and even semester exams are in May/June. Schedule is announced by JNTUH.

question: What is a supplementary examination?
answer: A supplementary exam is an additional opportunity for students who fail in one or more subjects to clear the backlog. It is conducted by JNTUH after semester results.

question: What documents are needed for hall ticket?
answer: Students must have 75% or more attendance (or valid condonation)
column4: and no active disciplinary issues to receive hall ticket.

question: What is the dress code at GNITS?
answer: On regular days students should wear salwar kameez or churidar with dupatta. Jeans and T-shirts are permitted on Saturdays (non-formals day).

question: Are mobile phones allowed in classrooms?
answer: No. Mobile phones are strictly not allowed inside classrooms and laboratories. Violations result in phone confiscation.

question: What is JNTUH?
answer: JNTUH stands for Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University Hyderabad. GNITS is affiliated to JNTUH and all B.Tech exams are conducted by JNTUH.

question: What is CBCS?
answer: CBCS stands for Choice Based Credit System. It is the academic system followed by JNTUH affiliated colleges including GNITS.

question: How many total credits are needed for B.Tech?
answer: Approximately 160 credits are required to complete B.Tech degree from JNTUH.

answer: The odd semester typically begins in the last week of August and runs until November/December.

question: Can I change my room in the hostel?
answer: Room change requests can be submitted to the warden. Changes are accommodated subject to availability and valid reasons.

question: What if there is a medical emergency in the hostel at night?
answer: Contact the warden on duty immediately. The warden has emergency contacts and ambulance arrangements. Security is also available 24/7 at 040-23004199.

question: What is the GNITS email domain?
answer: GNITS email addresses use the domain @gnits.ac.in.

question: Does GNITS have any rankings?
answer: GNITS is NAAC accredited and is recognized among the top women's engineering colleges in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh.

question: How many students are enrolled at GNITS?
answer: GNITS has approximately 2000+ students across all programs and years.

question: What is the student to faculty ratio at GNITS?
answer: GNITS maintains a good student to faculty ratio across departments to ensure quality education.

question: Is there bus facility for day scholars at GNITS?
answer: GNITS has transport facilities. Contact the college administration for current bus routes and timings.

question: How do I get a duplicate ID card?
answer: Report to the student affairs office
column4: pay the duplicate ID card fee

question: What are the college holiday dates?
answer: GNITS follows JNTUH academic calendar. Key holidays include national holidays
column7: and other state holidays. Calendar published at start of year.

question: Does GNITS have a separate library for each department?
answer: Each department has a small departmental library in addition to the central library. The Central Library is the main resource center.

question: What is the fine if I write in a library book?
answer: Writing or marking in a library book results in a minimum fine of Rs. 100 plus damage assessment.

question: Can I access library resources from home?
answer: Some digital resources like N-LIST may require campus network. NPTEL is publicly accessible. Check with library for VPN access options.

question: What is the exam schedule for B.Tech?
answer: JNTUH releases the exam schedule on jntuh.ac.in approximately 3 to 4 weeks before exams. It is also displayed on the GNITS notice board.

question: How do I check my exam results?
answer: JNTUH results are published on jntuh.ac.in. Login with your hall ticket number to view results.

question: What is the process for re-evaluation of answer sheets?
answer: Apply for re-evaluation through JNTUH website within the notified period after results. A fee is applicable per subject.

question: Where can I find the JNTUH syllabus?
answer: JNTUH syllabus is available on jntuh.ac.in under academics section. It is also available from your department office.

question: Is there project work in B.Tech?
answer: Yes. B.Tech final year students undertake a major project worth 6 credits. Internal project and external viva are conducted.

question: What is the seminar requirement for M.Tech?
answer: M.Tech students are required to present seminars on technical topics as part of their academic evaluation.

question: Can day scholars use the library?
answer: Yes. All enrolled students including day scholars have library membership and can use all library facilities.

question: Is there a canteen inside the college for day students?
answer: Yes. The main college canteen serves all students including day scholars. Subsidized rates are available.

question: What should I do if I lose my college ID card?
answer: Report immediately to the student affairs office
column5: and collect your new ID card.

question: What is the process to get transcripts from GNITS?
answer: Apply for transcripts at the examination section in the administrative block with required fee. Processing takes 7 to 10 working days.

question: Can I transfer from another college to GNITS?
answer: Lateral entry transfers are possible subject to seat availability and JNTUH norms. Contact the admissions office for details.

question: Does GNITS have a placement cell WhatsApp group?
answer: Yes. Final year registered students are added to T&P Cell communication groups. Register with placement cell to be included.

question: What companies does GNITS have MoU with?
answer: GNITS has MoUs with several IT companies and industry partners for training
column4: and placements. Details available from T&P Cell.
